name: Pacific Northwest Fellowship
notes:
"PNWF is a fellowship rooted firmly in the Big Book of Alcoholics Anonymous and guided by the Twelve Traditions. This is a space where newcomers and old-timers alike can come together in unity, service, and recovery.

Our primary purpose is to stay sober and help other alcoholics achieve sobriety, just as outlined in the program of Alcoholics Anonymous. We read directly from the Big Book, share personal experience, strength, and hope, and keep our group grounded in the spiritual principles that have helped millions find freedom from alcoholism.

Though we meet online, we’re not just a digital group — we encourage members to connect locally as well."

"PNWF is a fellowship rooted firmly in the Big Book of Alcoholics Anonymous and guided by the Twelve Traditions. This is a space where newcomers and old-timers alike can come together in unity, service, and recovery.

Our primary purpose is to stay sober and help other alcoholics achieve sobriety, just as outlined in the program of Alcoholics Anonymous. We read directly from the Big Book, share personal experience, strength, and hope, and keep our group grounded in the spiritual principles that have helped millions find freedom from alcoholism.

Though we meet online, we’re not just a digital group — we encourage members to connect locally as well."

Meeting ID 836 5333 0521
Password: 061035

name: Wedgewood Men (Hybrid)
notes:
If joining in-person, please find us by walking through the rear parking lot and down the stairs.
We are a Step and Traditions study group, founded in 1950 and operating continuously since then. Our meetings consist of reflections on a single Step or Tradition each week, encouraging each member to speak about his personal experience, strength and hope with respect to that week's topic (or on something else that's on his mind, if that's the case). We welcome new members and would love to have you join us, in-person* or remotely.
In the interest of the health of our members and others who use this space, we'd appreciate it if you would: Wear a mask if you are not fully vaccinated, Wash or sanitize your hands upon entering,
Refrain from attending if you have been traveling within the last 10 days and are not fully vaccinated.

name: Grace Group
notes: Grace Group is a women's meeting of Alcoholics Anonymous. We meet Sundays and Wednesdays from 6-7 pm Pacific Time. Sundays are closed meetings; Wednesdays are open and are newcomer-focused. We study a rotating selection of AA literature, including pamphlets. Most meetings, we read from either "Alcoholics Anonymous," "The Twelve Steps and Twelve Traditions of AA," or "Living Sober." Sobriety milestones are celebrated the first Sunday of each month for the prior month.

name: Pacific Northwest Fellowship
notes:
"PNWF is a fellowship rooted firmly in the Big Book of Alcoholics Anonymous and guided by the Twelve Traditions. This is a space where newcomers and old-timers alike can come together in unity, service, and recovery.

Our primary purpose is to stay sober and help other alcoholics achieve sobriety, just as outlined in the program of Alcoholics Anonymous. We read directly from the Big Book, share personal experience, strength, and hope, and keep our group grounded in the spiritual principles that have helped millions find freedom from alcoholism.

Though we meet online, we’re not just a digital group — we encourage members to connect locally as well."

name: Pacific Northwest Fellowship
notes:
"PNWF is a fellowship rooted firmly in the Big Book of Alcoholics Anonymous and guided by the Twelve Traditions. This is a space where newcomers and old-timers alike can come together in unity, service, and recovery.

Our primary purpose is to stay sober and help other alcoholics achieve sobriety, just as outlined in the program of Alcoholics Anonymous. We read directly from the Big Book, share personal experience, strength, and hope, and keep our group grounded in the spiritual principles that have helped millions find freedom from alcoholism.

Though we meet online, we’re not just a digital group — we encourage members to connect locally as well."

name: It Works if You Work it (Big Book Study)
Pacific Northwest Fellowship
notes:
Open meetings are available to anyone interested in Alcoholics Anonymous’ program of recovery from alcoholism. Nonalcoholics may attend open meetings as observers.

"PNWF is a fellowship rooted firmly in the Big Book of Alcoholics Anonymous and guided by the Twelve Traditions. This is a space where newcomers and old-timers alike can come together in unity, service, and recovery.

Our primary purpose is to stay sober and help other alcoholics achieve sobriety, just as outlined in the program of Alcoholics Anonymous. We read directly from the Big Book, share personal experience, strength, and hope, and keep our group grounded in the spiritual principles that have helped millions find freedom from alcoholism.

Though we meet online, we’re not just a digital group — we encourage members to connect locally as well."

Meeting ID 836 5333 0521
Password: 061035

name: Bliss
notes:
Each meeting will have a topic from G.S.O.-approved literature, such as the Big Book, Daily Reflections, Dr. Bob and the Good Old Timers, and, of course, the 12 Steps and 12 Traditions book.

We will enjoy laughing with (not at) each other when we hear stories of what it was like, what happened, and what it’s like now. Playful banter in short quips will be acceptable.

We will ensure any Newcomers (or ‘transplants’ who moved into the area) leave with reliable resources based on our knowledge of the Pacific Northwest’s Clean & Sober communities.

This will be a safe meeting. Any ‘predatory behavior’ will not be tolerated. For your safety, we will be happy to walk you to your car if requested when you leave the 12 & 12.

During the last fifteen minutes of each meeting, we will make sure any Newcomers who haven’t had an opportunity to share will get the chance to do so.

We’ll make the coffee - please consider bringing some snacks to The 12 & 12 Fellowship Hall, 10011 Main Street, Bothell, Washington 98011
